title: Quadlet .build, allow setup context directory for custom location of containerignore files
body:
### Feature request description
podman version 5.5.2

Hello,

Working with quadlet found, if I setup the structure of the project like this:

```
app.py
avatar_user.jpg
container/{.containerignore,containerfile}
.env
.git
.gitignore
oauth2.py
pg_websocket.py
requirements.txt
users.json
```

and use this .build:

```
[Unit] Description=Build image for APP

[Build]
ImageTag=localhost/app:latest
ForceRM=true
SetWorkingDirectory=%h/app
File=container/containerfile

[Install]
WantedBy=default.target
```

It will ignore the .containerignore from the folder container.

It will be good have a param like IgnoreFile or ContainerIgnore.

Using PodmanArgs, I will get: unknown flag: --ignorefile container/.containerignore


### Suggest potential solution

Add ContainerIgnore or IgnoreFile, to podman quadlet .build template

### Have you considered any alternatives?

adding PodmanArgs="--ignorefile container/.containerignore", will fail with unknown flag: --ignorefile container/.containerignore


### Additional context

_No response_

comments.body: I think it looks like a bug. WDYT? @flouthoc  @nalind @mheon

Not a bug per the manpage of `podman build` which states that the ignore file must exist in the context directory - so the subdirectory holding .containerignore prevents its use by default.

That said, adding an Ignorefile option to Quadlet (or some similar, more descriptive name) seems like a good idea and should be quite simple. Tagging good first issue.

First, I agree that adding support for `IgnoreFile` key in `.build` files that translates to `--ignorefile` argument should be simple.

But, I do want to ask about:
> adding PodmanArgs="--ignorefile container/.containerignore", will fail with unknown flag: --ignorefile container/.containerignore

Where do you get this error? From Quadlet which in then fails the creation of the service unit. Or from Podman when running the service. I don't understand why it does not work

> Where do you get this error? From Quadlet which in then fails the creation of the service unit. Or from Podman when running the service. I don't understand why it does not work

In quadlet, what I share is an actual quadlet command that I tried to use like alternative and won't work correctly.

The `.build` file you shared does not include the `PodmanArgs` key, so, I can't know for sure what you are running. I used the following:
```
$ cat /tmp/build-ignore/foo.build
[Unit]
Description=Build image for APP

[Build]
ImageTag=localhost/app:latest
ForceRM=true
SetWorkingDirectory=%h/app
File=container/containerfile
PodmanArgs="--ignorefile container/.containerignore"

[Install]
WantedBy=default.target

$ QUADLET_UNIT_DIRS=/tmp/build-ignore/ /usr/libexec/podman/quadlet -dryrun
quadlet-generator[237451]: Loading source unit file /tmp/build-ignore/foo.build
---foo-build.service---
[Unit]
Wants=network-online.target
After=network-online.target
Description=Build image for APP
SourcePath=/tmp/build-ignore/foo.build
RequiresMountsFor=%t/containers

[X-Build]
ImageTag=localhost/app:latest
ForceRM=true
SetWorkingDirectory=%h/app
File=container/containerfile
PodmanArgs="--ignorefile container/.containerignore"

[Install]
WantedBy=default.target

[Service]
WorkingDirectory=/tmp/build-ignore
ExecStart=/usr/bin/podman build --force-rm --tag localhost/app:latest --file container/containerfile "--ignorefile\x20container/.containerignore" %h/app
SyslogIdentifier=%N
Type=oneshot
```

Can you please share the `.build` file with the `PodmanArgs` that reproduces the `unknown flag` error?

Oh, I didn't share because like it was a feature request, but simple add the line I share and won't work

That's what I did and it did work for me. So, there's something different between what we did and I'm trying to find out what it is. Can you please share your file?

```
[Unit]
Description=Build de la imagen Docker para la API APP

[Build]
# Etiqueta de la imagen resultante
ImageTag=localhost/app:latest

# Elimina las capas intermedias después del build para ahorrar espacio

# Directorio raíz

# Nombre del archivo de construcción
File=container/.containerfile

# Archivo ignore
#PodmanArgs="--ignorefile container/.containerignore"

[Install]
WantedBy=default.target

```
/usr/lib/podman/quadlet -dryrun -user ~/.config/containers/systemd
```
---app-build.service---
[Unit]
Wants=podman-user-wait-network-online.service
After=podman-user-wait-network-online.service
Description=Build de la imagen Docker para la API
RequiresMountsFor=%t/containers
SourcePath=/opt/APIs/app/.config/containers/systemd/app.build

[X-Build]
# Etiqueta de la imagen resultante
ImageTag=localhost/app:latest

# Elimina las capas intermedias después del build para ahorrar espacio
ForceRM=true

# Directorio raíz
SetWorkingDirectory=%h/api-rest-app

# Nombre del archivo de construcción
File=container/.containerfile

# Archivo ignore
PodmanArgs="--ignorefile container/.containerignore"

#GlobalArgs="--ignorefile container/.containerignore"

[Install]
WantedBy=default.target

[Service]
WorkingDirectory=/opt/APIs/app/.config/containers/systemd
ExecStart=/usr/bin/podman build --force-rm --tag localhost/app:latest --file container/.containerfile "--ignorefile\x20container/.containerignore" %h/api-rest-app
SyslogIdentifier=%N
Type=oneshot

```

systemctl --user start app-build.service 
```
Job for app-build.service failed because the control process exited with error code.
See "systemctl --user status app-build.service" and "journalctl --user -xeu app-build.service" for details.
```

```
× app-build.service - Build de la imagen Docker para la API app
```

found that quoting (`"`) is the issue. @ygalblum @Honny1 @mheon 

PodmanArgs="--ignorefile container/.containerignore" < Fail

--- have to use the absolute path
PodmanArgs=--ignorefile %h/api-rest-app/container/.containerignore < Work


 **It would be good put in the main documation (https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman-systemd.unit.5.html) in h1 or h2: "Don't quote the value and/or params"**

Thanks for checking again.
First, the error message is not from Quadlet but from Podman (even if the issue originates in Quadlet).

Second, the issue is not with quotes perse, but with the space escaping within the quotes. The following works for me:
```
PodmanArgs="--ignorefile=%h/api-rest-app/container/.containerignore"
```

The issue seems to start here: #25722.
@Luap99 @mheon WDYT?

> "--ignorefile container/.containerignore"

That is a single argument, I don't see how escaping the space would be related. It works just like your shell would, if you try ` podman build "--ignorefile container/.containerignore" ...` on the cli it fails as well because that is not a single, you need to pass then as two arg so `PodmanArgs="--ignorefile" "container/.containerignore"` should work as well.
That we turn the space into `\x20` makes no practical difference for this case since in the systemd unit the argument is still quoted so it will still be treated as one.

Adding IgnoreFile as official key makes sense of course.

> Adding IgnoreFile as official key makes sense of course.

For sure, this will solve this specific case. But, I'm trying to also look at the bigger picture.

IIUC you're saying that Podman can handle `"--arg=val"` or `"--arg" "val"`, but can't handle `"--arg val"`.
I'm not saying this needs to be addressed in podman, but maybe explained in `PodmanArgs`
Now regarding `IgnoreFile`, I think Quadlet should pass the value as is, right? How does `podman build` handle relative path? Is it relative to the context dir?
Yes pass the values as is. AFAIK the relative path is relative to the cwd and there is no special logic to resolve that relative to the context directory.
The relative to context directory is only when no --ignorefile is given that it looks for `.containerignore` in the context directory not the cwd.
Thanks. I've opened a PR for adding the support. I added a comment in the man page about relative path needing WorkingDirectory
